{{Short description|Physical constant relating temperature to energy at the particle level}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Boltzmann constant
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Statistical mechanics]], [[Thermodynamics]], [[Kinetic theory of gases]], [[Entropy]]
| symbol = k, k_B
| value = 1.380649×10⁻²³ J/K (exact by definition)
| dimension = [Energy] / [Temperature]
}}

The '''Boltzmann constant''' (<math>k</math> or <math>k_B</math>) is a fundamental [[physical constant]] that relates the average relative [[kinetic energy]] of particles in a [[gas]] to the [[thermodynamic temperature]] of the gas. It is named after the Austrian physicist [[Ludwig Boltzmann]], who made foundational contributions to [[statistical mechanics]].

Since 2019, the Boltzmann constant has an exact defined value:
:<math>k_B = 1.380649 \times 10^{-23}\ \text{J/K}</math>

== Fundamental role ==

=== Kinetic theory ===
{{Main|Kinetic theory of gases}}
In the [[kinetic theory of gases]], the average translational kinetic energy of a gas molecule is directly proportional to the absolute temperature:
:<math>\langle E_k \rangle = \frac{3}{2}k_B T</math>

=== Ideal gas law ===
The [[ideal gas law]] can be expressed microscopically:
:<math>PV = Nk_B T</math>

The macroscopic [[molar gas constant]] <math>R</math> is related to the Boltzmann constant by:
:<math>R = N_A k_B</math>
where <math>N_A</math> is the [[Avogadro constant]].

=== Statistical mechanics ===
{{Main|Statistical mechanics}}
In statistical mechanics, the Boltzmann constant appears in the [[Boltzmann factor]]:
:<math>P_i \propto e^{-E_i / k_B T}</math>

And in the definition of [[entropy]]:
:<math>S = k_B \ln \Omega</math>
where <math>\Omega</math> is the number of [[microstate (statistical mechanics)|microstates]]. This formula, engraved on Boltzmann's tombstone, bridges the microscopic and macroscopic worlds.

=== Equipartition theorem ===
The [[equipartition theorem]] states that each quadratic degree of freedom in a system at thermal equilibrium has an average energy of <math>\frac{1}{2}k_B T</math>.

== Historical definition (2019 redefinition) ==
Before 2019, the Boltzmann constant was measured experimentally. In the [[2019 redefinition of the SI base units]], it was given an exact fixed value, which defined the [[kelvin]] in terms of energy.

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Entropy]]
* [[Ideal gas law]]
* [[Kinetic theory of gases]]
* [[Laws of thermodynamics]]
* [[Statistical mechanics]]
* [[Temperature]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Physical constants]]
[[Category:Statistical mechanics]]
[[Category:Thermodynamics]]
[[Category:Ludwig Boltzmann]]
